cot_content:
Okay, so the question is about how to tell right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are A and B, which both involve tying a knot on the left earbud but at different ends.

First, I need to recall the design of typical earbuds. Earbuds usually have a speaker part that goes into the ear canal and a connecting wire that goes to the device. The left and right earbuds are often symmetrical in shape, making it hard to tell them apart just by looking at them.

The idea is to create a marker that helps identify the left one. Tying a knot on one side would create a physical difference. The key is where to tie the knot so that when you put them in your ears, you can feel the knot without looking.

Option A says to tie the knot at the connecting end of the left one. The connecting end is the part that's attached to the wire going to the device. If you tie a knot there, when you wear the earbuds, the knot would be near the wire, maybe behind the ear or along the neck. It might be harder to feel that knot when you're wearing them.

Option B suggests tying the knot at the speaker end of the left one. The speaker end is the part that goes into the ear. If you tie a knot there, it would be on the part that's inserted into the ear. When you put the earbuds in, you would feel the knot on the left side, helping you identify which one is left without looking.

So the answer is likely B because tying the knot on the speaker end (the part that goes into the ear) would make it noticeable when you wear them. The knot at the connecting end (option A) might not be as useful since it's not in the part you insert into your ear.
label: majority
